acceptance_criteria_for_revision: operations_note.md contains a numeric sensitivity analysis with recalculated totals for each scenario.
A concise decision table is present and maps each hourly recommendation to numeric inputs.
CSV header mapping is explicitly documented or CSV updated to canonical headers.
Grader metadata contains a single rubric_item_scores block, no duplicated/conflicting pass/fail flags, and final_score consistent with rubric item scores.
verification_steps_after_revision: Confirm operations_note.md includes labeled sensitivity analysis and decision table and re-run artifact preview to ensure non-truncated visibility.
Run deterministic precheck and model grader again to ensure rubric_item_scores are produced once and consistently.
Confirm verifier no longer flags internal consistency issues and that ri_4 is marked passed when sensitivity analysis and decision table are present.

planned_changes: Update operations_note.md: add a 'Numeric sensitivity analysis' section with at least two scenarios (hydrogen price +/-10% and energy intensity +/-10%) and full recalculation of per-hour and cumulative totals for each scenario.
Add a compact decision table to operations_note.md with columns: hour | recommended action | governing rule(s) | renewable_MW | grid_price_usd_per_MWh | break-even price used.
Explicitly document the CSV header mapping in operations_note.md (one-line mapping) and optionally update electrolyzer_dispatch.csv to use canonical header names expected by automated verifiers.
Add an explicit electricity_consumed_kWh (or electricity_consumed_kWh_per_hour) line/column or clearly state the conversion used (1000/52 kg per MWh and kWh/kg = 52) to make verifier checks trivial.

Normalize grader metadata: ensure a single rubric_item_scores block is produced, reconcile passed_criteria and failed_criteria so they are consistent, and ensure final_score equals the weighted aggregation of the authoritative rubric block.
Attach the full (non-truncated) operations_note.md to the grader input so the model/verifier can see the sensitivity analysis and decision table during automated checks.

Include a short reproducibility snippet (code or command) in operations_note.md that shows how the CSV was generated (e.g., the node snippet or a one-block Python script) to help re-computation by verifiers.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Resolve documentation gaps (missing visible numeric sensitivity analysis and decision table) and remove grader metadata inconsistencies so verifier can mark ri_4 as passed.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Artifacts and numeric calculations are present and reproducible, but the grader output contains internal inconsistencies (contradictory pass/fail flags and duplicated rubric entries) and the operations_note.md preview does not clearly include the required numeric sensitivity analysis and a concise decision table. To pass verification, normalize grader metadata and augment documentation with a numeric sensitivity scenario (e.g., +/-10% hydrogen price and energy intensity) and a compact decision table mapping each hour to the recommended action and governing inputs.
